blocks.body: Result: so / therefore / as a result shows you how to express consequence without mixing an everyday connector with more formal options.

{"level":"B1-B2","intro":"When one event becomes the result of another, we often use so, therefore, or as a result. All three show consequence, but they do not build the sentence in the same way.","rules":[{"label":"So = direct everyday result","color":"emerald","text":"So is very common in speech and directly links the cause to the consequence.","example":"It was raining, so we stayed home."},{"label":"Therefore = more formal","color":"blue","text":"Therefore often sounds more logical and formal, especially in writing.","example":"The road was closed. Therefore, we took a different route."},{"label":"As a result = clear outcome phrase","color":"amber","text":"As a result works as a ready-made phrase for a final consequence.","example":"He missed the bus. As a result, he arrived late."}]}

blocks.heading: 1. How so works
blocks.body:
So most often joins two parts inside one sentence.

  • I was tired, so I went to bed early.
  • The shop was closed, so we bought nothing.

It is a very natural choice in spoken and neutral English.

blocks.heading: 2. How therefore and as a result work
blocks.body:
• Therefore often stands at the start of a separate sentence or after a semicolon: The evidence was weak; therefore, the case collapsed.
  • As a result often introduces the final outcome: The team trained hard. As a result, they played confidently.

Both options usually feel more formal than so.

blocks.heading: 3. Punctuation and pattern
blocks.body:
• so often stands between two parts of one sentence, sometimes after a comma.
  • therefore often takes a comma after it when it starts the sentence: Therefore, ...
  • as a result is also often followed by a comma at the start of a new sentence.

These are not just synonyms - they fit into sentence structure in different ways.

blocks.heading: 4. Common mistakes
blocks.body:
• Mistake: using therefore exactly like a simple conjunction instead of giving it the punctuation it needs.
  • Mistake: doubling the logic: because ..., so ... in one very simple structure often sounds overloaded.
  • Mistake: choosing a very formal therefore where plain so would sound more natural.

blocks.heading: 5. Quick rule
blocks.body:
• So = the most natural everyday way to show result.
  • Therefore = a more formal logical transition.
  • As a result = a ready-made phrase for an outcome.
  • Choose the word by pattern and formality, not only by meaning.
